response.reasoning: Step 1 — Experience years: Freelance from 2006, first formal role 2009, giving ~17 years of professional software engineering. Meets the 15-year minimum. Step 2 — Education: Two bachelor's degrees (CS and Fine Arts/Photography). No master's or PhD, and nothing AI-specific. The preferred advanced AI degree is not met. Step 3 — AI expertise: Current role at re:cinq (April 2025–present, ~1 year) involves AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows. This is hands-on and current but very recent and narrow in scope; there is no demonstrated track record of building or shipping AI-powered products at scale, no publications, no recognized industry presence in AI. Step 4 — Cloud: Uses AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker across roles. Competent practitioner, but no evidence of cloud platform strategy, cloud architecture at enterprise scale, or deep cloud advisory experience. Step 5 — Entrepreneurial: Founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, still active), built CMSushi, multiple open-source libraries. Builder mentality is genuine and sustained. Step 6 — Seniority level: Titles are Senior/Staff Software Developer. No director-level titles, no P&L ownership, no org-level strategy mandate. The role explicitly targets a director-level IC who shapes AI strategy and advises large strategic customers. Step 7 — Strategic advising and thought leadership: Gave a talk at FOSS4G 2019 and ran local workshops. Meaningful community engagement, but far below the scale of recognized industry expert advising enterprise partners on AI adoption. Step 8 — Builder/code fluency: Genuinely broad — TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Swift, C++, embedded, mobile, GIS. Strong technical generalist. Step 9 — Decision: Candidate is a capable, broad, entrepreneurially-inclined engineer with nascent AI involvement, but lacks the AI depth, strategic advisory experience, and director-level seniority the role requires.
strengths: ~17 years of broad, polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Genuine entrepreneurial track record: founded and maintains GISCollective, authored multiple open-source libraries across different stacks
Recent hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq, showing active ramp into the AI space
concerns: No advanced degree in an AI-related field; education is two bachelor's degrees with no AI or ML coursework visible
AI experience is limited to ~1 year and is infrastructure-level tooling, not recognized expertise with shipped AI products or published research
No evidence of director-level scope, enterprise customer advisory, or strategic thought leadership at the scale this role demands
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong, experienced engineer with genuine breadth and an entrepreneurial streak, but this role is a director-level AI strategy position requiring recognized AI expertise, enterprise advisory experience, and the ability to shape product and go-to-market strategy at scale. His AI work is real but only ~1 year old and implementation-focused rather than strategic. The gap between his current profile and the role's seniority and AI-leadership expectations is substantial.
